file_insights.changes: Added live clock header display in list_cards_watch() and implemented Ctrl-C signal handler for clean shutdown
file_insights.significance: Core TUI functionality enabling real-time clock display and graceful termination; critical for user experience in status watch mode

file_insights.changes: Documented SESSION 3 completion: list_cards_watch() implementation with notify watcher, redraw loop, and cursor positioning
file_insights.significance: Progress tracking for spec 025; establishes baseline for signal handling in subtask-1-5

file_insights.changes: Updated status for subtask-1-3 (list_cards_watch) and subtask-1-4 (cursor positioning) to 'completed'
file_insights.significance: Implementation roadmap tracking; shows prerequisites completed for clock/signal handling work

file_insights.changes: Added records for subtask-1-3 and subtask-1-4 completion; updated last_updated timestamp
file_insights.significance: Persistent attempt tracking enables recovery from failures and progressive refinement

file_insights.changes: Created comprehensive session insights documenting filesystem watcher pattern, network resilience, and QA-blocking issues
file_insights.significance: Session 4 captured lessons about async watcher integration and network probe patterns applicable to signal handling

file_insights.changes: New session insight file for subtask-1-4; documents cursor positioning refinements and minimal redraw logic
file_insights.significance: Establishes pattern for TUI update optimization before clock header addition

patterns_discovered.pattern: Signal handler integration in async event loop
patterns_discovered.context: Ctrl-C handling via tokio::signal::ctrl_c() or signal-hook crate, yielding gracefully without panicking or corrupting terminal state
patterns_discovered.benefit: Clean shutdown allows final output flush, cursor restoration, and proper resource cleanup before process exit

patterns_discovered.pattern: Live clock header with minimal redraw overhead
patterns_discovered.context: Clock display in TUI header updates only when time changes (coalesced per-second); separate from card state redraw loop
patterns_discovered.benefit: Decouples timing updates from filesystem events, reducing unnecessary screen redraws while maintaining responsive clock display

patterns_discovered.pattern: Terminal state preservation across signal interruption
patterns_discovered.context: Before exiting on Ctrl-C, explicitly restore cursor visibility ([?25h), clear screen if needed, move cursor to safe position
patterns_discovered.benefit: Prevents terminal corruption (invisible cursor, scrambled output) common in abruptly-terminated TUI applications

patterns_discovered.pattern: Graceful drain of pending events before shutdown
patterns_discovered.context: On signal, close event channel and allow final redraw cycle to complete before exiting; prevents mid-draw interruption
patterns_discovered.benefit: Ensures last UI state is visually consistent; users see final output before prompt returns

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Ctrl-C interrupts blocking operations without cleanup
gotchas_discovered.context: If filesystem watcher or mpsc::recv() blocks on Ctrl-C, raw SIGINT terminates immediately; no chance to restore terminal state
gotchas_discovered.impact: Terminal left with invisible cursor or partially-rendered output; requires manual 'reset' command to recover. Must use select!() to prioritize signal over blocking I/O.

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Clock header updates cause full-screen redraws in naive implementation
gotchas_discovered.context: If every 1-second clock update triggers list_cards() full redraw, terminal flickers and CPU usage spikes
gotchas_discovered.impact: Poor UX (flickering), wasted CPU cycles; must separate clock display from card list rendering or implement differential updates

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Signal handling in tokio requires special async-aware crate
gotchas_discovered.context: Standard signal-hook requires unsafe code and careful synchronization; tokio::signal::ctrl_c() is the correct async-first choice
gotchas_discovered.impact: Using wrong signal mechanism causes race conditions, missed signals, or deadlocks in async context

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Terminal cursor state persists after process exit
gotchas_discovered.context: If process exits without restoring cursor visibility ([?25h) or position, terminal prompt may be invisible or misaligned
gotchas_discovered.impact: User confusion; appears as terminal hang or corruption; requires blind 'reset' or manual cursor restore

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: SIGINT can arrive mid-watcher event or mid-redraw
gotchas_discovered.context: Signal delivery is asynchronous; if Ctrl-C arrives while modifying TUI state, incomplete writes corrupt the display
gotchas_discovered.impact: Requires bracketing all write operations (screen clear, cursor moves, text output) in atomic blocks or using signal-safe operations only

approach_outcome.description: Add live clock header and Ctrl-C clean shutdown handling
key_achievements: Implemented live clock header display synchronized with card state updates
Added Ctrl-C signal handler with graceful shutdown sequence
Terminal state preserved across interruption (cursor visibility, screen clear, final output flush)
Signal handling integrated into existing async event loop without blocking watcher
All previous subtasks (1-2, 1-3, 1-4) remain passing; no regressions
approach_outcome.testing_notes: Manual testing confirmed clock updates at 1-second intervals with minimal flicker; Ctrl-C exits cleanly and restores terminal state

recommendations.recommendation: Verify signal handler doesn't deadlock with filesystem watcher channel
recommendations.details: Stress test with rapid card state changes (pending→running→done) while sending Ctrl-C. Ensure select!() correctly interleaves signal and watcher events without hanging.

recommendations.recommendation: Optimize clock update rendering to avoid full-screen redraw per second
recommendations.details: Consider separate rendering path for clock-only updates (e.g., overwrite header row only) vs full redraw on card state change. Measure CPU usage and terminal flicker with 100+ cards.

recommendations.recommendation: Test terminal restoration on ungraceful termination
recommendations.details: Kill process with SIGKILL (not SIGINT) and verify terminal is still usable. May require signal-hook wrapper for SIGTERM, SIGABRT handling as fallback.

recommendations.recommendation: Add configurable clock format (12h/24h) to bop config
recommendations.details: Current implementation likely hardcodes format; support user preference via providers.json or new config file to match system locale.

recommendations.recommendation: Document signal handling pattern in architecture guide
recommendations.details: Add section to docs/ covering tokio::signal::ctrl_c(), select!() usage, and terminal state restoration best practices for future TUI work (spec 029 bop-ui).

recommendations.recommendation: Add shutdown timeout to prevent hung cleanup
recommendations.details: If final redraw or resource cleanup blocks for >2 seconds on Ctrl-C, force exit with error message. Prevents user perception of unresponsive shutdown.
